label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is about why Americans don't take all their vacation days. The options are A, B, C, D. Let me read through the passage again to find the answer. The first paragraph says that more than half of Americans don't take all their vacation days even though they think they need more holidays. Then it mentions that some check emails while on break. Samuel Nahmias talks about the US economic situation and unemployment rates. He says that with 9.6% unemployed, people with jobs are unwilling to take time off. The passage also compares to European countries where more vacation time is normal, but in the US, many have only 6-10 days or less. The reasons given for not taking time off include too many responsibilities, stress, and not having time to plan. Also, technology allows them to stay connected, so they feel they need to check in. So the main reasons mentioned are the fear of losing their job because unemployment is high (so they don't want to be away), and the pressure to stay connected via technology. The question asks what's the reason. The options are: A) they devote themselves to work (workaholics) B) they are afraid they may lose their jobs C) they are asked to keep in touch with bosses D) they aren't in need of holidays The passage says "people who have jobs seem to be unwilling to take time off" because of the high unemployment rate. So they're worried about their jobs. Also, the answer B seems to fit. The majority mentioned responsibilities and stress, but the
